stemHtml: Looking at a Low-Level Significant Weather Chart (LL SWC) with isobars, how do the isobars usually appear when representing a ridge?

options.a: Convex outward from the anticyclone, extending away from the high-pressure centre.
options.b: Bulging outward from the anticyclone, becoming rounder toward the periphery.
options.c: Curved around the periphery of a cyclone, near the frontal boundaries.
options.d: Their pattern is irregular and cannot be determined.
